inputs.label: Fixed Costs
inputs.businessContext: Total fixed costs (e.g., rent, salaries, insurance) per period. Based on cost accounting standards (e.g., GAAP, IFRS).

inputs.label: Variable Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Cost that varies with production volume (e.g., materials, labor). Standard in managerial accounting.

inputs.label: Selling Price per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Revenue per unit sold. Must exceed variable cost for positive contribution margin.

inputs.label: Time Period
inputs.businessContext: Period for which fixed costs are defined. Aligns with financial reporting periods.

inputs.label: Target Profit
inputs.businessContext: Desired profit above break-even. Used for target volume calculations.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data (e.g., based on historical accuracy). Adjusts results for uncertainty.

thresholds.contributionMargin: if contributionMargin <= 0 then 'CRITICAL: Selling price does not cover variable cost. No break-even possible.'
thresholds.breakEvenUnits: if breakEvenUnits > 1000000 then 'WARNING: Break-even volume is very high. Consider reducing fixed costs or increasing price.'

hiddenLossDrivers: if contributionMargin <= 0 then 'Negative contribution margin: product is not viable.'
if breakEvenUnits > 1000000 then 'Extremely high break-even volume may indicate unrealistic pricing or cost structure.'
suggestedActions: If break-even volume is too high, consider reducing fixed costs (e.g., renegotiate rent, outsource).
If contribution margin is low, evaluate increasing selling price or reducing variable costs (e.g., better supplier deals).
Use target profit analysis to set sales goals.
